sections.question: whether a person who does not appear on the ballot is considered a candidate and must file campaign statements
sections.conclusion: Section 82007 of the Government Code defines the term candidate as 'an individual who is listed on the ballot ... or who receives a contribution or makes an expenditure or gives his consent for any other person to receive a contribution or make an expenditure with a view to bringing about his nomination or election to any elective office ...'. Thus, the candidates described in your letter will not have to file campaign statements if they do not appear on the ballot and do not receive contributions or make expenditures.
sections.question_synthetic: The County Clerk of Inyo County asked whether candidates who do not appear on the ballot but are appointed to office under the Uniform District Election Law are required to file campaign statements under the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to issue a formal opinion because the question did not raise a substantial question of interpretation. However, they clarified that candidates not appearing on the ballot are not required to file campaign statements unless they receive contributions, make expenditures, or give consent for others to do so.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a declination letter (formal opinion denied) but includes substantive guidance. The document includes both the incoming request letter and the FPPC response. Very clear question and conclusion sections present.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: whether a person who does not appear on the ballot is considered a candidate and must file campaign statements

CONCLUSION: Section 82007 of the Government Code defines the term candidate as 'an individual who is listed on the ballot ... or who receives a contribution or makes an expenditure or gives his consent for any other person to receive a contribution or make an expenditure with a view to bringing about his nomination or election to any elective office ...'. Thus, the candidates described in your letter will not have to file campaign statements if they do not appear on the ballot and do not receive contributions or make expenditures.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declined to issue a formal opinion to the Inyo County Clerk regarding whether appointed candidates (under the Uniform District Election Law) must file campaign statements, but provided guidance that such candidates need not file if they do not appear on the ballot and do not receive contributions or make expenditures.
